rulings.Case Title: PARKFRONT INVESTMENTS LLC VS. KOKKARI LTD.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 22, 2026. Line 1. RESPONDENT KOKKARI LTD. , A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney'S Fees is continued to February 11, 2026 for opposing party to deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to department 501 no later than February 2, 2026 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. (moving and reply received). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: JOSHUA BERLINER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For An Order To Compel Mediation And Award Costs And Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 22, 2026. Line 2. PETITIONER JOSHUA BERLINER, AURORA KURLAND Notice Of Hearing On Petition For An Order To Compel Mediation And Award Costs And Attorneys Fees is continued to February 25, 2026. No later than February 11, 2026 Petitioner to file a supplemental brief not to exceed 2 pages, providing citations (no argument) to cases granting petitions to compel mediation under CC&Rs or any other contracts involving a mediation clause. rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, AS TRUSTEE VS. 644 BROADWAY, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Prior Order; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Request For Judicial Notice In Support; Declaration Of Glen H. Olives In Support Of Motion (Motions)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 22, 2026. Line 5. OTHER TERRY MOLLICA (NON-PARTY), GLEN OLIVES Motion For Reconsideration Of Prior Order; is GRANTED as follows: Motion to Quash is set for hearing on January 22, 2026. It should not have been taken off September 12, 2025 calendar and should have been continued to be heard with the Motion to Compel Turnover of Funds. Motion to Quash is Denied. Motion to Compel Turnover of Funds was personally served on Mr. Olives and sub-served on Mr. Mollica per the proofs of service filed on September 30, 2025. The Court notes that by the time the Motion to Compel Turnover was heard on November 19, 2025, Motion to Quash was stale as it was filed months before the service reflected in the September 30, 2025 proofs of service. Any issues with jurisdiction could have been and were addressed in the October 27, 2025 Opposition to the Motion to Compel Turnover of Funds. Those arguments have been considered in the Court's ruling. Motion for Reconsideration of the order on the Motion to Compel Turnover of Funds is Denied. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: NEVILLE WRIGHT VS. TURK STREET INVESTORS LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 22, 2026. Line 7. DEFENDANT TURK STREET INVESTORS LLC MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT is GRANTED with leave to amend. Request for punitive damages is stricken. Plaintiff shall have 10 days from notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint stating facts (not conclusions) supporting a request for punitive damages against a corporate defendant. Plaintiff must specify which cause of action Plaintiff is seeking punitive damages under. Plaintiff shall not list "Punitive Damages" as a separate cause of action as is currently stated in the Third Amended Complaint. If Plaintiff fails to timely amended the complaint, Defendant shall file an answer to the Third Amended Complaint (subject to this order). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: DAVID CARRASCO VS. CYNTHIA CARRASCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 22, 2026. Line 11. DEFENDANT CYNTHIA CARRASCO MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS is OFF CALENDAR.
